task_completion.description: Set Tor Browser Security Level to 'Safest'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Tor Browser shield icon menu or the Privacy & Security settings page shows the Security Level set to 'Safest'.
The Tor Browser address bar displays 'check.torproject.org' or the main browser window shows the Tor Project connection check page.

task_completion.description: Configure browser to clear history on shutdown and enable automatic private browsing
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Tor Browser Privacy & Security settings show 'Always use private browsing mode' and 'Clear history when Tor Browser closes' as checked, OR the about:config page shows 'privacy.clearOnShutdown.history' and 'browser.privatebrowsing.autostart' set to true.

task_completion.description: Clear all browsing history, download history, active logins, cookies, cache, and offline website data for 'Everything'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Clear All History' dialog box is visible with the Time range to clear set to 'Everything', and the checkboxes for Browsing & Download history, Active Logins, Cookies, Cache, and Offline Website Data are all checked.

task_completion.description: Capture a screenshot of the current state to the Desktop
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'incident_screenshot.png' is visible on the Desktop, in a file manager navigating to /home/ga/Desktop, or open in an image viewer.

task_completion.description: Save an incident report to the Desktop containing 'LOCKDOWN COMPLETE' and the current date
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows the creation or content of 'incident_report.txt' on the Desktop containing the exact text 'LOCKDOWN COMPLETE' along with a date.

integrity.description: Verify that the Tor Browser GUI was used to change settings and clear history
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the actual Tor Browser application open and being interacted with, rather than just terminal commands modifying prefs.js or sqlite databases directly.

integrity.description: Verify the screenshot is a genuine image file
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshot file should be generated using a screenshot tool or appear as a valid image (e.g., image thumbnail or open in an image viewer), not a dummy file created with touch or echo.

integrity.description: Verify files are saved to the exact requested paths
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshot and report files must be explicitly saved to /home/ga/Desktop/, not the home directory or Documents folder.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target files are exactly /home/ga/Desktop/incident_screenshot.png and /home/ga/Desktop/incident_report.txt. The report must contain 'LOCKDOWN COMPLETE' and the current date. The browser must have visited check.torproject.org. The required browser preferences are privacy.clearOnShutdown.history=true and browser.privatebrowsing.autostart=true. The security level must be set to 'safest'.
